title: rosa × dumetorum var. puberula
intro: Rosa × dumetorum var. puberula, also known as the downy wild rose, is a shrub that belongs to the family Rosaceae and is characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and cancer. The health benefits of Rosa × dumetorum var. puberula include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and promote relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
